message:
fix(events): sort extracted object keys before event identity hash + ft-gc4hz circuit-breaker test

Two related stability fixes + tracker updates:

## events.rs: event identity key must ignore extracted object insertion order (ft-8uj10)

event_identity_key() folded the detection's `extracted` serde_json::Map
into its hash by iterating obj directly. serde_json::Map preserves
insertion order (it's a wrapper around IndexMap by default), so two
detections that carry the same set of key/value pairs but were
constructed with keys in different orders would produce DIFFERENT
identity keys. That breaks deduplication: the same logical event
slipping in from two pipelines that assembled the extracted object in
different order would dedupe-to-two instead of dedupe-to-one.

Fix: materialise the obj into a Vec<(&str, &Value)>, sort by key (raw
string compare — stable for the ASCII keys we use in practice), and
iterate the sorted sequence. Zero behavior change for single-source
pipelines that always emit keys in a consistent order; fixes the
dedupe hole for any consumer that joins events from heterogeneous
producers.

Inline note kept concise: the sort is on a local Vec of borrowed
references, so the source obj is untouched — downstream code that
reads extracted afterward still sees the original insertion order.

Add `event_identity_key_ignores_extracted_object_insertion_order`
regression test that builds two Detections with the same logical
content but inserts (agent, retry_after) vs (retry_after, agent) and
asserts the identity keys match.

## retry.rs: regression test for ft-gc4hz circuit-breaker cancellation exemption

The ft-gc4hz fix in 503c9506 added the Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) match
arm to with_retry_and_circuit_cx but shipped without a targeted
regression test — only the retry module's existing happy-path
coverage. Close that gap:

`cancelled_error_does_not_trip_circuit_breaker_ft_gc4hz` constructs a
circuit with failure_threshold=1 (the minimum; any recorded failure
trips it), runs a single operation that returns
Err(Error::Cancelled("caller cancelled".into())), and asserts:
  - the result propagates unchanged as Error::Cancelled
  - circuit state is still CircuitStateKind::Closed
  - consecutive_failures == 0
  - telemetry().failures_recorded == 0 (AND .successes_recorded == 0)
  - a subsequent real Ok(7) operation still runs and returns 7

The final Ok(7) check is the practical contract the fix protects:
before ft-gc4hz, a single cancellation would open the breaker and
the next unrelated caller would get CircuitOpen instead of their
actual result.

## Beads

- File flywheel_connectors-8uj10 (alternate id ft-8uj10) already filed
  in the prior commit; this one is about frankenterm, closed status
  now reflected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-0vt3k","title":"[SEV] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology rollback check only compares pane id set, not split geometry","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology Phase 5 (rewritten in 30172171 to fix ft-olq2e) now derives post_rollback_pane_ids from server.registry().snapshot() and asserts the sorted id list matches baseline_pane_ids. This is stronger than the previous vacuous baseline-JSON roundtrip, but it still only validates the *set* of surviving pane ids, not the restored split tree geometry: split ratios, split directions (horizontal vs vertical), parent/child edges, pane widths/heights, or the z-order of panes within the topology. A broken rollback that restores the correct 2 pane ids but swaps their split direction, scales their sizes, or re-parents them under a different split node would still pass this assertion. Metamorphic invariant: TopologySnapshot::from_registry(post_rollback) == TopologySnapshot::from_registry(pre_expansion) — not currently asserted. Fix by capturing a full TopologySnapshot before Phase 3 expansion, then after rollback compare structural fields (split tree, ratios, per-pane dimensions) in addition to pane id set. The existing 'restored_topo = from_json(&initial_topo_json)' on line 422 still only proves serde roundtrip; it does not compare to the live post-rollback state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:42.691288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912477Z","close_reason":"Resolved in 4784c64f + d29498d6. d29498d6 added TopologyFingerprint covering HSplit/VSplit direction, per-split child ratios (encode_ratio to u16), per-leaf pane_id/rows/cols, tab_id, active_pane_id, window_id, and active_tab_index; Phase 5 now asserts post_rollback_fingerprint == initial_fingerprint AND != expanded_fingerprint, so a rollback that restores the correct pane id set but swaps split direction, rescales panes, or re-parents nodes fails. 4784c64f tightened the check further with a direct TopologySnapshot equality (post_rollback_topo == &initial_topo) as the primary assertion and baseline-JSON == initial_topo as the secondary. Ran the test at HEAD: binary compiles clean; runtime verification on local host blocked by macOS dyld bottleneck under concurrent-agent compile load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-12e8l","title":"[SEV] mux-server Alert-after-PaneRemoved re-inserts HashMap entry, permanent leak","description":"crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs:256 processes MuxNotification::Alert by calling handler.per_pane(pane_id), which routes to SessionHandler::per_pane at sessionhandler.rs:282. That function uses HashMap::entry(..).or_insert_with(..) — a get-or-insert. When a MuxNotification::Alert arrives for a pane whose entry was already cleared by Item::Notif(MuxNotification::PaneRemoved) at dispatch.rs:247, the or_insert_with re-creates a fresh PerPane entry. The pane is already gone from mux, so no subsequent PaneRemoved will fire for that pane_id — the entry is a permanent leak.

Concrete interleaving:
  1. Mux fires PaneRemoved(1) followed by (or interleaved with) a delayed Alert{pane_id:1, PaletteChanged} that was enqueued by a config-reload path or a termwiz late-palette flush.
  2. Dispatch loop processes PaneRemoved -> handler.remove_per_pane(1) clears the map entry (line 247).
  3. Dispatch loop processes Alert -> handler.per_pane(1) -> or_insert_with -> re-creates an entry (line 256).
  4. Line 262 schedule_pane_push(1) likewise calls per_pane() internally, reinforcing the stale entry.
  5. No further PaneRemoved for pane 1 is ever emitted (pane already killed). Entry leaks for the lifetime of the ClientSession.

Severity: unbounded growth proportional to (alerts-after-removal × lifetime). Long-lived mux-server clients (editor sessions, persistent SSH mux) with high pane churn accumulate stale entries. Each entry holds a PerPane with notifications Vec, RenderableDimensions, and SurfaceCopy — not large individually but multiplied by churn + uptime, it matters.

Fix: expose a non-inserting accessor SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present(pane_id) -> Option<Arc<Mutex<PerPane>>> and use it for the Alert arm. If the pane isn't tracked, drop the Alert (the client can't render a dead pane anyway). Keep the or_insert_with pathway for client-initiated PDU arms (SendKeyDown, SendPaste, etc.) where insertion on first reference is correct. Also skip the schedule_pane_push for a pane that was just removed (line 262) — same reasoning.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:46:06.681760Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407372Z","close_reason":"Completed: added SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present non-inserting accessor and routed dispatch.rs Alert arm through it. Alert-after-PaneRemoved now drops the stale alert silently instead of re-creating a permanent map entry. Code changes landed in commit 3b45408d (another agent's codec-fuzz commit bundled my dispatch.rs + sessionhandler.rs edits into their push — my 37 lines are present in HEAD). cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --offline passed clean in 13.28s during my local verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.28","title":"[gui][test] GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite","description":"# GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite

## What
Automated tests for the wgpu rendering pipeline: correctness, visual regression detection, and performance validation.

## Why
GPU rendering bugs are invisible to unit tests — they produce wrong pixels, not wrong values. We need screenshot-based comparison to catch rendering regressions in text layout, cursor position, selection highlighting, split pane borders, and floating pane overlays.

## Tests

### Unit Tests (no GPU required)
- `test_cell_vertex_generation` — cell (row, col) produces correct vertex positions
- `test_atlas_rect_lookup` — glyph lookup returns correct UV coordinates
- `test_atlas_eviction_lru` — LRU evicts least-recently-used glyphs
- `test_dirty_rect_merge` — adjacent dirty cells merge into single rect
- `test_dirty_rect_clear_on_present` — dirty rects cleared after frame

### Visual Regression Tests (headless GPU or software renderer)
- `visual_basic_text` — "Hello, World!" renders correctly
- `visual_unicode_cjk` — CJK characters render at double width
- `visual_emoji` — emoji renders correctly (with fallback glyph)
- `visual_box_drawing` — box-drawing characters connect seamlessly
- `visual_cursor_shapes` — block, bar, underline cursor shapes
- `visual_selection_highlight` — selected text has correct background color
- `visual_split_pane_borders` — split pane borders render as clean lines
- `visual_floating_pane` — floating pane renders with shadow/border above tiled panes
- `visual_50_panes` — 50 split panes all render legible text

### Performance Tests
- `perf_frame_time_single_pane` — full-screen pane, measure frame time (target: < 4ms)
- `perf_frame_time_50_panes` — 50 panes, measure frame time (target: < 16ms)
- `perf_atlas_warmup` — time to cache 200 unique glyphs (target: < 100ms)
- `perf_scroll_fps` — continuous scroll, measure sustained fps (target: 60fps)

### E2E Script: `scripts/e2e_gpu_rendering.sh`
```
1. Launch frankenterm-gui in headless mode (--headless)
2. Open shell, run: echo "RENDER_TEST_$(date +%s)"
3. Capture screenshot to /tmp/ft_visual_test.png
4. Compare against golden screenshot (perceptual hash, tolerance for font differences)
5. Open 10 split panes, capture screenshot
6. Verify all 10 panes contain visible text (OCR or pixel sampling)
7. Measure frame time over 100 frames, report p50/p95/p99
8. Report: pass/fail with visual diff images on failure
```

## Golden Screenshot Management
- Golden screenshots stored in `tests/golden/gpu/`
- Updated via `BLESS=1 cargo test` (like insta crate)
- Platform-specific goldens: `tests/golden/gpu/macos/`, `tests/golden/gpu/linux/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Visual regression suite runs in CI headlessly
- Frame time p99 < 16ms for single pane
- Font atlas handles full Unicode BMP without crash
- Visual diff images attached to failing CI runs for diagnosis","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:37.252309Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:38:44.195459Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","gui","rendering","testing"],"comments":[{"id":2635,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"RoseCoyote","text":"RoseCoyote taking a bounded support slice under the blocked GPU regression-suite bead: expand pure renderer helper/unit coverage in existing test modules (`termwindow/webgpu.rs` and `termwindow/render/mod.rs`) without pretending the full headless screenshot suite is ready before `ft-1memj.10` stabilizes. Goal: tighten edge-case coverage around surface-format/extent selection and render color/cursor helper invariants so the eventual visual-regression harness has a firmer correctness floor.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:45:45Z"},{"id":2637,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"RoseCoyote","text":"RoseCoyote progress: added focused renderer helper edge-case tests in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs. New coverage pins alpha-mode empty-capability fallback, per-axis surface extent clamping, explicit palette-vs-style foreground precedence, bright ANSI palette handling under bold intensity, and reverse-video cursor exact-threshold semantics. Validation: `cargo fmt --check` and `git diff --check` passed. `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-gui termwindow::webgpu::tests` failed on worker `vmi1293453` with `RCH-E307` because `x11.pc` is missing from the worker image; companion `termwindow::render::tests` run fell open local after `RCH-E324` and then stalled in an uninterruptible wait, so I terminated that process rather than leave a useless local compile hanging. Bead remains in_progress because the full GPU regression suite still depends on ft-1memj.10 and proper worker images.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:52:53Z"},{"id":3521,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook taking a bounded ft-1memj.28 slice while Agent Mail is timing out under DB contention. Scope: audit the existing GPU renderer helper test surface in crates/frankenterm-gui and land a non-overlapping correctness/regression slice that improves renderer invariants without pretending the full headless visual suite is ready.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:27:47Z"},{"id":3526,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook support-slice update: added a resize-path surface-extent helper in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs so live resize keeps zero-size minimize semantics but clamps oversized usize dimensions instead of truncating them into wrapped u32s. Added focused tests for zero-preserving resize extents plus large and independent-axis clamping. Remote targeted verification reached real compilation on worker vmi1149989 but failed before test execution because the worker image is missing x11-xcb.pc for crate x11. Local rustfmt --check on the touched file and git diff --check are clean.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:37:10Z"},{"id":3527,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook support-slice update: added a resize-path surface-extent helper in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs so live-resize keeps zero-size minimize semantics but clamps oversized usize dimensions instead of truncating them into wrapped u32s. Added focused tests for zero-preserving resize extents plus large/independent-axis clamping. Verification: remote targeted lane  reached real compilation on worker vmi1149989 but failed before test execution because the worker image is missing  for crate  (-I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libx11/1.8.13/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/xorgproto/2025.1/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxcb/1.17.0/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxau/1.0.12/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxdmcp/1.1.5/include -L/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libx11/1.8.13/lib -lX11-xcb -lX11 -L/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxcb/1.17.0/lib -lxcb failed). Local non-cargo hygiene is clean: rustfmt --check on the touched file and git diff --check pass.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:38:28Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-1p752","title":"[testing-fuzzing] crash-detector fuzz target for frankenterm-escape-parser Parser::parse raw bytes","description":"# Motivation

`frankenterm_escape_parser::Parser::parse(&[u8], callback)` is the
state machine that consumes untrusted terminal output for every
captured pane. The crate has 2 existing fuzz targets:

  * `termwiz_csi_parser.rs` — structure-aware around CSI sequences
  * `osc_marker_parser.rs`  — structure-aware around OSC markers

Neither is a plain raw-bytes crash detector. State-machine paths
that the structure-aware generators don't reach (SOS/PM/APC,
partial DCS/Sixel streams, intermediate-byte boundary transitions,
pathological ESC chains) go uncovered.

# Scope

New fuzz target `fuzz/fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs` that feeds
arbitrary bytes into `Parser::parse` with a no-op callback, asserts
no panic / no infinite loop / no allocator blow-up. Size-bounded at
64 KiB to keep iteration latency low (matches the existing raw-bytes
targets in this repo).

# Invariants (beyond crash-freedom)

1. `parse` consumes all bytes it's fed (state machine never strands).
2. The callback is invoked at most some finite number of times per
   input byte (linear, not quadratic).
3. `parse_as_vec` round-trips `parse` — calling both on the same
   input produces identical Action counts.

# Seed corpus

Empty + minimal ESC + malformed-CSI + OSC-with-nulls + APC + nested
DCS partial stream. 4-5 seeds.

# Build

    cargo +nightly fuzz run escape_parser_raw -- -runs=100000

Targets 2000+ exec/s given escape parsing is hot-path byte-driven
code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:39.779511Z","created_by":"pane3-escape-fuzz","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:39.779511Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["escape-parser","fuzz","testing"]}

The earlier fuzz target shipped in c93fa5f7 hit a libfuzzer-sys SDK-version mismatch on mac and was removed in 5c470432; the proptest provides the same invariant coverage without the link issue.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["escape-parser","fuzz","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","title":"Publish user-facing release/tuning guidance for different hardware tiers","description":"Provide clear guidance on expected behavior, config knobs, and troubleshooting paths across low/mid/high-end hardware.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Publish hardware-tier guidance backed by validated unit/integration/e2e/perf evidence from the resize/reflow program.
2. Guidance must include explicit operational playbooks, rollback/fallback steps, and diagnostics workflows for each tier.
3. Require structured logging/diagnostics artifact references so operators can map symptoms to recommended actions quickly.
4. Validate guidance via scripted end-to-end operator journeys and update docs when discrepancies are discovered.
5. Closure requires auditable documentation package with evidence links and owner sign-off.","notes":"Dependency rationale: user-facing tuning guidance depends on controlled beta results so recommendations reflect observed real-world smoothness and artifact behavior across hardware tiers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.550243Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:33.109635Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:33.108768Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging","track-rollout","user-impact","ux"],"comments":[{"id":334,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","author":"OliveDesert","text":"OliveDesert progress (2026-02-20): drafted user-facing hardware-tier guidance doc at docs/resize-user-facing-release-tuning-guidance-wa-1u90p.8.5.md covering low/mid/high expectations, recommended tuning profiles (Conservative/Balanced/Performance), release channel posture, troubleshooting commands, fallback triggers, and release-notes template. Added cross-link from docs/resize-rollout-plan-wa-1u90p.8.2.md so rollout documentation chain now references this guidance.","created_at":"2026-02-20T01:55:29Z"},{"id":2624,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","author":"CobaltCanyon","text":"CobaltCanyon starting a bounded docs/ops slice. Agent Mail bootstrap/registration is currently blocked by the same transient MCP DB error other agents have recorded, so this bead thread is the durable coordination record for now. Changes landed in docs/resize-user-facing-release-tuning-guidance-wa-1u90p.8.5.md, docs/tuning-reference.md, docs/operator-playbook.md, and docs/frankenterm-gui-user-guide.md. Scope of this slice: normalize the current bead ID in the guidance title/body, add explicit 'how to use this guide' positioning, and wire discoverability links from the tuning reference, operator playbook, and GUI fleet guide so operators can actually find the release-posture + fallback document during incidents or rollout decisions. Validation: git diff --check on the four touched files.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:13:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-26738","title":"restart_scheduler: clamp negative hours_since_last in recency_penalty (LOW)","description":"Fixed in d327d7c1. recency_penalty returned negative values when hours_since_last was negative (clock regression via NTP / VM rewind / manual date reset), propagating through score_window and corrupting SchedulingDecision.windows ranking. Benign in production (negative scores always below min_score_threshold so no trigger fires) but breaks decision-surface invariants. Fix clamps to 0 (treat as just restarted). 2 regression tests pin the invariant. Found during subsystem bug sweep 2026-04-21 (workflows/policy/safety/release-gates, pane6 review).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:27.355801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892323Z","close_reason":"Fixed in d327d7c1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2guux","title":"reality-check gaps","description":"Reality-check sweep of the repo (pane 5, session 2026-04-20) against
README.md + AGENTS.md + docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md. Most
capability claims match the code; the four material gaps below are
worth tracking. No fixes this tick — documentation-only finding.

Methodology: read the README "Supported Surface Matrix" and "Quick
Example", then grep the CLI command tree at `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`,
the MCP tools at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs`, the web
endpoints at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/router.rs`, and the
runtime layer at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` +
`runtime_compat.rs`.

Findings (severity ranked):

G1 [MEDIUM] Watch-daemon asupersync claim vs actual reactor dependency
  Evidence:
    - README.md:38-41 "runtime model is asupersync-native",
      "runtime_compat remains as an audited boundary".
    - crates/frankenterm-core/tests/runtime_labruntime.rs:13-18
      explicit comment:
        "Full ObservationRuntime integration tests (startup,
         shutdown, discovery, capture) require the runtime_compat::task
         module to be migrated from tokio::spawn to asupersync::spawn.
         Until that migration completes, ObservationRuntime::start()
         cannot run under asupersync RuntimeFixture because task::spawn
         → tokio::spawn panics without a tokio reactor."
    - runtime.rs:184 uses `crate::runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx`
      which still goes through the tokio-backed path when the
      `asupersync-runtime` feature is disabled.
  Why it matters: the primary operator surface (`ft watch`) cannot be
  exercised end-to-end under a pure asupersync runtime fixture today.
  The README is honest that runtime_compat is "an audited boundary";
  the specific gap is that the watch daemon's task spawns transit
  that boundary. Tracked by the ft-xbnl0.2.x epic but worth surfacing
  as a doc clarification on README.md:38-41 ("native with a named
  quarantine").

G2 [MEDIUM] `ft restart` is Unix-only, not disclosed in the support matrix
  Evidence:
    - README.md:124 "Session persistence | Supported with backend
      prerequisite | Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and
      `ft session doctor` are implemented".
    - crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27032 emits:
        "ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms."
      and exits 1 on non-Unix; the full `execute_restart_workflow`
      call path sits inside `#[cfg(unix)]` at line 27036.
  Why it matters: a Windows operator following the README's
  session-persistence story will hit this only at runtime.
  Supportable via an asterisk in the matrix or an explicit
  "Unix-only" caveat in the `ft restart` section.

G3 [LOW] Performance claims lack linked artifacts
  Evidence:
    - README.md:51 "<50ms lag" for delta extraction.
    - README.md:62 "keeps 200+ panes under 1GB vs 4GB+".
    - README.md:113 "TOON reduces token consumption by 40-60%".
    - No matching benchmark artifact path in docs/ referenced from
      these claims; the verification-contract doc at docs/ft-xbnl0-
      verification-contract.md mandates Level C artifact bundles for
      soak/perf-adjacent claims but the README does not cite any.
  Why it matters: performance claims presented as current fact in
  the README should either cite an artifact bundle or be softened to
  "target" / "design goal" until evidence lands. Non-blocking.

G4 [LOW] `SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT = 200` silently bounds OSC133 state
  Evidence:
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:188
        `const SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT: usize = 200;`
    - mcp.rs:288-310 `derive_osc_state_from_storage` walks only the
      last 200 segments. If OSC133 markers span further back, the
      derived state is silently incomplete; the helper returns
      `Ok(None)` with no gap marker.
  Why it matters: a quiet-pane edge case (no activity for >200
  segments) would yield an incorrect alt-screen / prompt state via
  the MCP surface without a visible warning. Worth either documenting
  the bound or recording a `markers_unknown_due_to_window_limit`
  signal.

Non-gaps verified present and matching claims:

  - `ft robot state` / `state --include-text`: main.rs:2375-2391
  - `ft robot search --mode {lexical,semantic,hybrid}`:
      main.rs:4633-4711 enum + From impl; semantic/hybrid properly
      gated behind `semantic-search`/`frankensearch` features.
  - `ft mcp serve`: main.rs:33124
  - `ft web` + `/stream/events` + `/stream/deltas`:
      web/router.rs:65,70 — streaming exists; README's
      "more than 'health only'" claim is accurate.
  - `ft distributed agent`: main.rs:165-168 under
      `#[cfg(feature = "distributed")]`.
  - `ft session doctor`, snapshot save/restore/list/inspect/diff:
      SnapshotCommands + SessionCommands enums defined around
      main.rs:4207.
  - `ft tx run`/`tx rollback`/`tx plan`/`tx show`: main.rs:21114+
      via `execute_tx_run_with_executor`.
  - Policy "21 subsystems": unified_telemetry.rs:242 comment; actual
      PolicyEngine struct at policy.rs:3466-3517 declares ~22
      subsystem-shaped fields. Close-enough; no drift worth fixing.
  - tokio/smol usage in supported core: grep finds zero production
      call sites outside `runtime_compat` + `dependency_eradication`
      + `forbidden_dep_guards` (guard files are intentional).

Documented only. No shippable fix this tick per /reality-check-for-project
ground rules. Related: AGENTS.md rule zero (NEVER delete
frankenterm-core) is intact; the four gaps above are additions to the
README, not subtractions from the code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:01:41.726765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.915672Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.913552Z","close_reason":"Reopening-in-error by p5-orch robot-triage pick — the bead was already closed with the four reality-check gaps (G1 runtime_compat quarantine language, G2 ft restart Unix-only caveat, G3 softened perf/token claims, G4 OSC/OSC133 marker window bound) addressed across two commits:

 {"id":"ft-azjol","title":"[sec] ipc.rs: non-constant-time token compare (HIGH)","description":"CVSS v3.1 AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N ~5.7. Fixed in 5810a3a4. IpcAuth::authorize() used String::eq which short-circuits on first byte mismatch, plus .find() which short-circuits on first candidate match. Both are exploitable via timing side-channel over Unix socket: co-tenant process can recover the stored IPC token byte-by-byte AND distinguish token position in the vec. Fix: constant-time byte XOR accumulator in ipc_constant_time_eq + explicit loop in authorize() that visits every candidate. 7 regression tests. Found during /security-audit-for-saas adapted for CLI control-plane (pane6 phase C).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:27.060545Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:31.327952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:31.327699Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5810a3a4","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b5rbd","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:04.554054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945232Z","close_reason":"dup of ft-htf0i","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062383Z","close_reason":"Added timeout/disconnect/cancel-style readback coverage and hardened the helper deadline with checked_add","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}

 {"id":"ft-cbeyl","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist silently drifts when tool renamed or removed","description":"In commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface),
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:254-316 hard-codes
three whitelists of tools/resources/templates expected to be db-gated:

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [
    \"wa.accounts\",
    \"wa.events\",
    \"wa.reserve\",
    \"wa.release\",
    \"wa.search\",
    \"wa.send\",
    \"wa.workflow_run\",
] {
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

The assertion only checks absence from \`no_db_tools\`. If a tool is
renamed (e.g., \`wa.accounts\` → \`wa.list_accounts\`) or removed entirely,
the whitelist entry silently stays true (\`!no_db_tools.contains(\"wa.accounts\")\`
is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and
the template whitelist (lines ~295, \`wa://accounts/{service}\` etc.).

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could
accidentally become always-on (or be removed from db-gated registration)
without the test catching it.

Fix: for each whitelist entry, assert \`full_tools.contains(entry)\`
BEFORE checking \`!no_db_tools.contains(entry)\`. That way, if the
whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test
fails and forces whoever made the change to update the whitelist
deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {
    assert!(
        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"whitelist drift: {expected_absent_tool} no longer exists in db-backed manifest — update the test\"
    );
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

Severity rationale: medium — test-quality issue that masks regressions
in MCP tool surface categorization. Not a correctness bug in production
code.

Evidence: commit 1bde42b3 diff lines 262-316 of the new
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` test body.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915565Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915363Z","close_reason":"Added full_*.contains(X) pre-assert for each whitelisted tool/resource/template before the !no_db.contains(X) check. If a whitelisted identifier disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test now fails instead of passing trivially. Fix landed in 4d52f550. Verified 6/6 tests pass via rch exec.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","test-gap","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0","title":"[EPIC] End-to-end swarm integration proof: demonstrate full observe-detect-react loop with live agents","description":"# Goal
Prove that FrankenTerm actually delivers on its core value proposition end-to-end: observing live AI agent panes, detecting state transitions, triggering automated workflows, and exposing actionable data through Robot Mode. Today every subsystem has unit tests, but nobody has proven the aggregate works.

# Background
The reality check (2026-04-06) found that individual subsystems are well-implemented:
- Ingest pipeline: real delta extraction with 4KB overlap matching
- Pattern detection: real Aho-Corasick + Bloom + regex
- Storage: real SQLite v23 + FTS5
- Robot Mode: fully implemented
- Policy engine: 40+ subsystems
- Fleet memory: 4-tier backpressure (Normal/Elevated/Critical/Emergency)

But there is NO evidence that someone has run: ft watch -> observed real panes -> detected a real pattern -> triggered a real workflow -> verified via ft robot. The individual subsystems are proven; the integration is not.

# Why This Matters
FrankenTerm promises a swarm-native terminal platform. Without E2E proof, it is a collection of well-tested libraries, not a platform.

# Architectural Intent
1. Start with a minimal proof: 3 panes, synthetic agent output, verify Robot Mode sees detection events.
2. Scale to realistic: 20 panes with mixed agent types, workflow triggers, policy gates, FTS search.
3. Scale to stress: 50+ panes with fleet memory engaged, prove tiered scrollback works under load.
4. Prove tx execution: ft tx run against real panes with policy gates and compensation.
5. For each scale tier, demonstrate the FULL loop: observe -> detect -> react -> audit.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Minimal E2E (ft-d0ez0.1): ft watch + 3 panes + pattern detection + Robot Mode queries
2. 20-pane integration (ft-d0ez0.2): multi-agent detection, workflow, policy, FTS search
3. 50-pane stress (ft-d0ez0.3): fleet memory pressure transitions (Normal->Elevated->Critical), tiered scrollback eviction
4. Tx E2E (ft-d0ez0.4): ft tx run with real pane operations, compensation, audit trail
5. All tests produce structured JSON artifacts for reproducibility.
6. Tests run in CI or have clear manual reproduction instructions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:35.214581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893210Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","goal-line","integration","proof","swarm"]}

 {"id":"ft-l6v1r","title":"[mor:distributed-systems] missed PaneDestroyed leaks tier/state maps across the swarm","description":"# Observation

`PaneTierClassifier` holds `RwLock<HashMap<u64, PaneState>>`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/pane_tiers.rs:238). Entries are added in
`register_pane` and removed in `unregister_pane`. The only thing
driving `unregister_pane` is a PaneDestroyed event flowing through the
native-events channel.

That channel has three failure modes documented in
`dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_cx`:
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Sent` — happy path
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Backpressure` — timeout expired, event dropped
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Closed` — channel gone

Backpressure drops are observable (ft-0e179 covers that), but a
dropped PaneDestroyed event is silently swallowed: the pane's entry
stays in the tier map forever. In a fleet with aggressive pane churn
(200+ panes, short-lived workflow agents), this is an unbounded leak
— map grows monotonically, classify() iterates more entries, the
RwLock hot path gets slower.

# Question

Should PaneTierClassifier have a reconciliation sweep that runs
periodically — say every 60s — and reconciles its HashMap against
`wezterm cli list`'s source-of-truth set of active pane IDs,
unregistering anything that wezterm no longer has? This converts a
silent-forever leak into an eventually-consistent cleanup, at the
cost of one extra `wezterm cli list` per sweep (already cached in
`PaneListCache`).

Same leak shape likely exists in other per-pane maps
(backpressure.paused_panes as Vec<u64>, aegis_backpressure state, any
per-pane metric stores). A crate-wide audit with one shared reconciler
is probably cheaper than 12 one-off fixes.

# CAP stance

Current design is "consistency by event delivery." On paper, the
event stream is authoritative. In practice, bounded queues mean
delivery is not guaranteed. A ground-truth reconciler trades the
"instantaneous" property for "eventually matches reality" — better
for a long-running daemon than the alternative.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:51.772093Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:30.788429Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:30.788109Z","close_reason":"Verified in d13fe125","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed-systems","mor","pane-lifecycle"]}
 {"id":"ft-l9mxa","title":"DistributedHttpClient response-read path does not observe mid-flight cx-cancel","description":"# DistributedHttpClient mid-flight cx-cancel gap

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 verification work. When a caller fires
`cx.cancel_with(...)` WHILE a `DistributedHttpClient::get` is in flight (blocked
reading response bytes from a stalled server), the cancel does NOT propagate to
the HTTP client's read loop.

## Reproduction
The ft-xbnl0.2.4 test
`distributed::tests::distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`
exercises this. Observed behavior:

    elapsed = 10.02s
    result = Err("deadline has elapsed at Time(10000000041ns)")

The elapsed = ~10s matches the test's DEFENSIVE outer timeout ceiling
(`crate::runtime_compat::timeout(10s, client.get(...))`), not the 50 ms
cx-cancel fire. The cancel went through (cx.is_cancel_requested() would return
true) but the HTTP client's await points didn't observe it.

## Relation to other findings
- **Tick 328** documented that `timeout_with_cx` observes cx *budget deadline*
  but not direct cx *cancel*. This is the same shape of gap at a different
  layer — the HTTP client wraps asupersync::http::h1 whose await points
  don't poll cx.
- **ft-kfkyi** (closed tick 351) was a distinct concern (3xx redirect
  exfil). This finding is lower-severity: not a security issue, just an
  operational-ergonomics gap.

## Impact
- Hung distributed RPC calls will eventually time out via internal limits
  (if any configured) but won't observe operator-initiated cancel.
- Agent workflows that fire cx.cancel to abort a slow peer call will wait
  the full internal-timeout window before the call returns.

## Fix approach (candidate paths)
1. Extend `DistributedHttpClient::get/post` to race the inner future against
   a cx-cancel future (tokio::select! or similar race).
2. Patch asupersync::http::h1 to thread cx through its await points and
   honor cancel at read/write boundaries. Bigger scope; upstream change.
3. Wrap the DistributedHttpClient inner calls in `timeout_with_cx` —
   but per tick 328, that only observes budget, not cancel. So this
   doesn't help unless the caller sets a short budget.

Option (1) is the narrowest change; aligns with the cx-first migration
pattern and doesn't require upstream asupersync work.

## Verification
The existing tick-380 snapshot test is the flip trigger. When the fix lands:
1. Observed elapsed drops from ~10s to << 10s (cx-cancel observed mid-read).
2. `result` shape changes from the outer-timeout Err to the HTTP client's
   own cancel-shaped Err.
3. Update the snapshot assertions to require the faster behavior.

## References
- Finding: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 (commit 4d925607)
- Related: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5` out-of-scope carve-out
  for primitive-doc-only coverage (timeout_with_cx / sleep_with_cx — ticks
  328 / 331).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:43.612639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342659Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 387 (commit 99640e41): DistributedHttpClient::{get,post} now race inner call against cx-cancel watcher (50ms poll). Mid-flight cancel surfaces as Ok(Err(Cancelled)) in ~70ms. Tick-380 snapshot test tightened to assert the new fast-cancel behavior (elapsed < 1s, exact Cancelled variant).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cx-cancel","distributed","http"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-l9mxa","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:58.856854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-lapw8","title":"Restore mux split wire JSON proptests dropped by 193a9ebb","description":"Commit 193a9ebb ("frankenterm-gui: saturate resize increment geometry") unintentionally deleted unrelated mux wire roundtrip coverage from frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs. Restore the deleted proptests and verify the mux serde test target still passes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:31.861035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:38.190544Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:38.190265Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in HEAD via commit a3409685 (2026-04-20).  {"id":"ft-lnlg4","title":"[reality-gap] tui/ftui_stub.rs named _stub but fully implemented (8296 LOC, header: 'no placeholders remain')","description":"# Code reality

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs`:
  - 8296 lines (non-trivial)
  - Header comment line 14: "All view bodies (Home, Panes, Search,
    Help, Events, Triage, History, Timeline) are backed by live
    state — no placeholders remain."
  - `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/mod.rs:103` declares
    `mod ftui_stub;` and re-exports `App, AppConfig, View,
    ViewState, run_tui` from it.

So the module name says "stub" but the header and the usage prove
it's the production FrankenTUI backend.

# Why this is a reality-check gap

Every sweep for placeholders/unimplemented code in this crate (this
one included) flags `ftui_stub` as a candidate for further
investigation, then discovers it's a false positive. Reality-check
sweeps burn cycles on this; code-review agents lose focus on real
gaps.

The naming is a legacy from when the file genuinely was a stub. The
content has grown into the real backend. The file name didn't
follow.

# Recommended disposition

```bash
git mv crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs \
       crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_backend.rs
```

…then update the one `mod ftui_stub` declaration and the two
`super::ftui_stub::` re-export lines. 15-second edit, preserves
git history via `git mv`.

# Risk

- `git mv` preserves blame + history.
- No external dependency on the file name (it's a sibling module).
- Existing grep-based tooling that searches for "ftui_stub" would
  miss it post-rename — worth a global grep + a deprecation comment
  left at `mod.rs` during the rename window.

# Severity

Lowest of the 5 reality-check gaps filed this pass (ft-z01cl,
ft-trby3, ft-6269x, ft-x4k2b are all higher-impact), but the
cheapest to fix. Good first-issue material.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:45:46.543886Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:54:09.847543Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:54:09.846972Z","close_reason":"Verified in a68246b7 (fix(tui): wire ftui_backend module rename follow-through) + 8c8f505b which completed the git mv ftui_stub.rs → ftui_backend.rs and updated mod.rs/keymap.rs/rollout.rs references.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hygiene","reality-gap","tui"]}
 {"id":"ft-lokam","title":"[LOW] diag bundle environment.json leaks absolute workspace cwd; tracing fields leak operator paths","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #6 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
Three logging/telemetry surfaces all come out clean on the obvious
secret-leak vectors (no bearer-token / API-key / pane-content
fields in tracing calls; no /etc/ or .ssh/ reads in diag
collection). One defense-in-depth gap remains: path privacy.

## Findings

### F1 [LOW] diag bundle environment.json includes absolute cwd
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic.rs:87-89
  ```rust
  cwd: std::env::current_dir()
      .ok()
      .map(|p| p.display().to_string()),
  ```
- Diag bundles (`ft diag bundle`) are often attached to bug reports
  and shared outside the host. An absolute cwd reveals:
  - the user's home path segment (`/Users/<username>/...` or
    `/home/<username>/...`),
  - the project path (`/Users/alice/projects/secret-startup-pitch-deck/`),
  - on some setups, mount points that hint at filesystem layout.
- Severity: LOW. Users implicitly share the workspace when
  debugging, but the current behavior removes the choice.

### F2 [LOW] tracing fields log operator-private paths
- Multiple `tracing::[info|warn|debug]!(..., path = %X.display(), ...)`
  call sites scatter socket paths, DB paths, and workspace paths
  through structured logs. Representative hits (non-exhaustive):
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:258, 302, 331,
      362, 401 — unix socket paths.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:1118 —
      `info_span!("telemetry_store_open", path = %db_path.display())`.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:1375, 2658, 2814 —
      socket paths on accept failures.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs:265 —
      `meta_path = %self.meta_path.display()`.
- Severity: LOW. These logs land in the user's local log file,
  not network-visible by default, but they do end up in diag
  bundles when `collect_recent_log_lines` is called.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- Bearer tokens in structured logs: grep across
  `tracing::{info,debug,warn,error}!` call sites for
  `token|bearer|secret|password|api_key` returned zero production
  hits. Clean.
- Pane content in tracing fields: no production code logs
  `segment.content`, `delta_text`, or `pane_text`. Only test
  `assert_eq!(segment.content, ...)` references found. Clean.
- Diag bundle reading privileged files: no `/etc/`, `.ssh/`,
  `.aws/`, `.gnupg/` reads in `diagnostic.rs`. The bundle only
  reads the ft SQLite DB (already redacted via `redact_events`)
  and writes to the workspace-local `diag_dir`. Clean.
- Environment variables in diag bundle: `gather_environment`
  captures only OS / arch / rust_version / wa_version / cwd —
  no `std::env::vars()` dump. Clean.
- Summarize_config: the docstring already calls out "no paths or
  secrets" and the implementation confirms — only numeric/boolean
  config values are copied over. Clean.

## Fix direction
- F1: replace absolute cwd with just the basename, or gate
  inclusion behind `DiagnosticOptions.include_cwd: bool` default
  `false`. Concretely at diagnostic.rs:87:
  ```rust
  cwd: if opts.include_cwd {
      std::env::current_dir().ok().map(|p| p.display().to_string())
  } else {
      std::env::current_dir()
          .ok()
          .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
  },
  ```
- F2: when a log line is included in a diag bundle via
  `collect_recent_log_lines` (or equivalent), run it through the
  existing `Redactor` path-aware rules. Non-bundle in-process logs
  stay as-is since they aid operator debugging on the local box.

## Severity rationale
- LOW (P2): neither finding reveals a secret, and both sit behind
  an operator action (`ft diag bundle` + sharing it). The fix
  shrinks the unintentional-share footprint but doesn't close a
  live exploit.

## Related
- ft-ybtyg (598a7c13) — snapshot/checkpoint hashing honesty.
- ft-2guux (doc reality-check) — complementary; this bead is the
  diagnostics-exfil counterpart.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:48:38.534325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:53:28.901879Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:53:28.901341Z","close_reason":"Closed by e790f481. F1 (cwd leak) fixed: DiagnosticOptions gains include_full_cwd:bool (default false). gather_environment now routes through new scrub_path_to_basename helper which returns only the final path component; opt-in via include_full_cwd:true restores absolute path for legitimate mount-point/multi-host repro cases. Both production entry points (generate_bundle + generate_bundle_with_cx) forward opts. 8 regression tests cover basename-only default, root/empty fallback to <scrubbed> sentinel, relative paths, opt-in restore, and the strongest guarantee: default environment.json output cannot contain /Users/ or /home/ segments. F2 (tracing field path-leak via collect_recent_log_lines) deferred as follow-up — requires routing log-line bundling through the existing Redactor infrastructure, out of scope for the 25-min cap but documented inline in the commit body for the next pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","diagnostics","logging","privacy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-ltpog","title":"native_events: decode errors logged at debug, invisible in production","description":"# [MEDIUM] native_events: malformed wire events logged at debug

**Subsystem:** native_events / protocol_recovery
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs (`handle_connection_with_cx`)

## The hole

When wire-event decoding fails (invalid JSON, bad base64, unknown event
type), the handler emitted:

\`\`\`rust
Err(err) => {
    debug!(error = %err, "failed to decode native event (cx path)");
}
\`\`\`

A malformed wire event is a protocol anomaly — version skew between
frankenterm and the mux-server, on-wire corruption, or a hostile peer
writing into the native-events socket. Operators routinely filter
`debug!` in production, so the signal was effectively lost.

Contrast with the `MAX_EVENT_LINE_BYTES` path a few lines above, which
already emits at `warn!`. Same class of anomaly, inconsistent level.

## Fix

Promoted the decode-error log to `warn!`. The `warn!` import was already
present — no dependency change.

Future work (separate bead): attach a telemetry counter for
decode-failure rate so it can drive a SLI rather than relying solely on
log sampling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:39.950825Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568595Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native-events","observability"]}
